senses.definition: People who oppose or reject traditional humanist ideas that place human beings and their values at the center.
examples: Some antihumanists criticize the idea that humans are the measure of all things.
Modern antihumanists often question the belief in stable human nature.

senses.definition: Opposed to humanitarian values such as kindness, protection of human life, or aid to people in need.
examples: Critics called the policy antihumanitarian because it harmed vulnerable refugees.
Many argued that cutting medical support was an antihumanitarian act.

senses.definition: People who oppose humanitarian values or actions, or who support policies that harm people in need.
examples: The protesters accused the leaders of being antihumanitarians.
Antihumanitarians often justify harsh treatment as necessary security measures.

senses.definition: A person who strongly opposes deception, trickery, or nonsense, especially in a moral or humorous way.
examples: He liked to describe himself as an antihumbuggist who hated all forms of cheating.
The columnist, a famous antihumbuggist, mocked dishonest advertising.

senses.definition: Someone who is strongly opposed to hunting animals, especially for sport.
examples: Antihunters argued that the bear hunt was cruel and unnecessary.
Local antihunters organized a campaign to ban trophy hunting.

senses.definition: Opposed to the hunting of animals, especially as a sport.
examples: The government faced pressure from antihunting groups.
New antihunting laws were introduced to protect endangered species.

senses.definition: The attitude of opposing hunting, or activities carried out to stop hunting.
examples: Antihunting has grown stronger in many European countries.
Her research focuses on the history of antihunting in rural areas.

senses.definition: Different forms or instances of opposition to hunting.
examples: Various antihuntings have emerged in response to large game safaris.
The book compares antihuntings in North America and Europe.

senses.definition: The antimatter form of hydrogen, made of a positron orbiting an antiproton.
examples: Researchers created and trapped antihydrogen atoms in the laboratory for several minutes.
Antihydrogen helps scientists test fundamental physical theories about matter and antimatter.

senses.definition: Atoms of antihydrogen considered as separate units or in different types or groups.
examples: The experiment produced several antihydrogens before the system was shut down.
Physicists compared antihydrogens with hydrogen atoms to measure tiny differences.

senses.definition: Reducing or counteracting hydrophobic (water-repelling) effects, especially in chemistry or materials science.
examples: The coating has antihydrophobic properties that improve its interaction with water-based solutions.
Researchers developed an antihydrophobic surface to enhance cell attachment.

senses.definition: Preventing or treating dropsy or abnormal fluid retention in body tissues (used in older medical language).
examples: The antihydropic remedy was commonly prescribed in the nineteenth century.
Doctors once relied on antihydropic drugs before modern diuretics were developed.

senses.definition: A drug or agent used to reduce dropsy or excess fluid in the body (in older medical usage).
examples: Antihydropin was prescribed to ease the patient's swollen legs.
Early physicians experimented with antihydropins of various strengths.

senses.definition: Not hygienic; likely to spread dirt, germs, or disease.
examples: Leaving food uncovered in the heat is highly antihygienic.
The inspector described the kitchen conditions as antihygienic and unsafe.

senses.definition: In a way that is not hygienic and may spread germs or disease.
examples: The tools were stored antihygienically in a dirty box.
Food was handled antihygienically, without gloves or washing.

senses.definition: A person who rejects materialism and denies that physical matter is the only reality (in philosophical discussion).
examples: As an antihylist, she argued that consciousness could not be reduced to matter.
Nineteenth-century debates often set hylists against antihylists.

senses.definition: Used to lower high blood pressure.
examples: She takes an antihypertensive drug every morning.
Antihypertensive therapy can greatly reduce the risk of stroke.

senses.definition: A drug that is used to lower high blood pressure.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antihypertensive to control his hypertension.
Different antihypertensives work in different ways on the body.

senses.definition: Drugs that are used to lower high blood pressure.
examples: Antihypertensives help prevent complications such as heart attacks and strokes.
Patients may need two or more antihypertensives to reach a safe blood pressure level.

senses.definition: Preventing or reducing sleep or the effects of hypnotic drugs.
examples: The stimulant has an antihypnotic effect and keeps patients awake.
Antihypnotic agents are sometimes used during surgery to maintain alertness.

senses.definition: A drug that reduces sleepiness or blocks the effect of sleeping medicines.
examples: The doctor administered an antihypnotic to reverse the sedative.
Researchers evaluated several antihypnotics in the clinical trial.

senses.definition: In a way that prevents or reduces sleep or the action of hypnotic drugs.
examples: The compound acts antihypnotically at low doses.
Taken late in the day, the medicine works antihypnotically and may cause insomnia.

senses.definition: Opposing or reducing excessive worry about having serious illnesses.
examples: The therapist used an antihypochondriac approach to challenge the patient's fears.
Some doctors recommend antihypochondriac counseling for anxious patients.

senses.definition: A person who opposes or criticizes excessive worry about illnesses.
examples: As an outspoken antihypochondriac, he warned against constant self-diagnosis.
His father, an antihypochondriac, dismissed minor complaints as unimportant.

senses.definition: The rhetorical practice of asking a question but refusing to answer it, or criticizing the technique of answering one’s own question.
examples: The speaker’s refusal to answer his own question was an example of antihypophora.
Some critics see antihypophora as a way to challenge manipulative rhetoric.

senses.definition: Calming or reducing extreme emotional excitement or hysteria.
examples: The nurse spoke in an antihysteric tone to soothe the panicked patient.
Antihysteric medication was given to control the outburst.

senses.definition: A drug used to reduce hysteria or extreme agitation.
examples: The doctor administered an antihysteric to help the patient relax.
Older antihysterics are now rarely used in modern practice.

senses.definition: Drugs that are used to reduce hysteria or extreme emotional agitation.
examples: In the past, doctors often relied on antihysterics to manage nervous conditions.
Modern psychiatrists tend to replace older antihysterics with safer medications.

senses.definition: Drugs that reduce inflammation, swelling, and pain in the body.
examples: Nonsteroidal antiinflammatories are commonly used to treat joint pain.
Some patients cannot take antiinflammatories because of stomach problems.

senses.definition: Reducing inflammation, swelling, or pain.
examples: She was given an antiinflammatory injection for her knee.
The cream has antiinflammatory properties that help soothe the skin.

senses.definition: A drug that reduces inflammation and pain.
examples: He took an antiinflammatory to ease his back pain.
Some antiinflammatories can irritate the stomach lining.

senses.definition: A person who opposes large established institutions, such as government bodies, churches, or big corporations.
examples: The poet was known as an antiinstitutionalist who distrusted all forms of authority.
Many young antiinstitutionalists rejected traditional political parties.

senses.definition: Opposed to large established institutions or formal structures of authority.
examples: The movement had a clear antiinstitutionalist ideology.
Their antiinstitutionalist stance appealed to many frustrated voters.

senses.definition: People who oppose or distrust large established institutions and formal systems.
examples: Antiinstitutionalists often criticize universities and government agencies.
Young antiinstitutionalists rallied against corporate power.

senses.definition: In a way that aims to prevent or suppress rebellion or violent uprisings.
examples: The government acted antiinsurrectionally, deploying troops across the capital.
Laws were enforced antiinsurrectionally during the period of unrest.

senses.definition: People who strongly oppose rebellions or violent uprisings and support maintaining the existing order.
examples: Antiinsurrectionists supported strict measures against the rebels.
The new laws were welcomed by antiinsurrectionists in parliament.

senses.definition: Opposed to Jacobin ideas or radical revolutionary politics, especially those linked with the French Revolution.
examples: The pamphlet took a strongly antijacobin position.
Antijacobin writers attacked what they saw as dangerous revolutionary ideals.

senses.definition: A person who opposed the Jacobins and radical revolutionaries during the French Revolution and later.
examples: British antijacobins feared that revolution would spread across Europe.
The novel portrays an antijacobin nobleman struggling with social change.

senses.definition: People or groups who opposed the Jacobins and radical revolutionary movements.
examples: Antijacobins published newspapers warning about the dangers of revolution.
The government quietly supported the antijacobins.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent or resist jamming of signals or equipment, especially in communications or radar.
examples: The military uses antijam technology to protect its satellite links.
Engineers developed an antijam system for the new radar.

senses.definition: Preventing or reducing the deliberate blocking of radio, radar, or other signals.
examples: The aircraft is equipped with antijamming devices.
Antijamming measures protect the navigation system from interference.

senses.definition: The protection of signals or systems against jamming.
examples: Modern satellites rely heavily on antijamming.
The project aims to improve antijamming for GPS receivers.

senses.definition: Different antijamming methods or systems.
examples: Researchers compared several antijammings for their effectiveness.
The report describes various antijammings used in modern warfare.

senses.definition: A trademark name for an old pain-relieving and fever-reducing medicine, no longer widely used.
examples: Antikamnia was advertised in the early twentieth century as a cure for many ailments.
Historical medical journals often mentioned antikamnia in treatment notes.

senses.definition: The part of an X-ray tube that is struck by electrons and gives off X-rays.
examples: The antikathode must be made of metal that can withstand high temperatures.
Early physicists studied how the antikathode produced different X-ray wavelengths.

senses.definition: An antitoxin that acts against kenotoxin, a type of bacterial poison (in older medical terminology).
examples: The laboratory produced an antikenotoxin for experimental treatment.
Physicians hoped that antikenotoxin would neutralize the infection.

senses.definition: A substance that prevents or reduces the formation of ketone bodies in the body.
examples: The study examined glucose as a possible antiketogen.
Doctors searched for effective antiketogens to treat diabetic complications.

senses.definition: The process of preventing or reducing the formation of ketone bodies in the body.
examples: Insulin plays a role in antiketogenesis by limiting fat breakdown.
The researchers focused on antiketogenesis in diabetic patients.

senses.definition: Preventing or reducing the formation of ketone bodies.
examples: Carbohydrate intake has an antiketogenic effect.
The antiketogenic properties of the drug were tested in animals.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent or punish illegal secret payments, especially in business or government contracts.
examples: The company implemented strict antikickback policies.
Antikickback laws forbid doctors from accepting payments for patient referrals.

senses.definition: A substance that blocks or reduces the action of kinases, enzymes that control many cell processes.
examples: Scientists tested a new antikinase as a possible cancer treatment.
The antikinase slowed down abnormal cell growth in the study.

senses.definition: Opposed to a king or to the idea of monarchy.
examples: The rebels spread antiking propaganda throughout the city.
Antiking sentiment grew after a series of scandals at court.

senses.definition: A person who opposes a king or the rule of kings.
examples: Antikings plotted to remove the monarch from power.
The antiking was exiled for speaking against the crown.

senses.definition: People or groups that oppose a king or monarchy.
examples: The antikings organized protests outside the palace.
Rulers often tried to silence antikings to keep control.

senses.definition: Reducing or preventing engine knocking (uneven burning of fuel in an engine).
examples: The fuel contains antiknock additives to improve performance.
Antiknock properties are important in high-compression engines.

senses.definition: A substance that reduces engine knocking when added to fuel.
examples: Lead compounds were once used as powerful antiknocks.
Newer antiknocks are designed to be less harmful to the environment.

senses.definition: Substances added to fuel to reduce engine knocking.
examples: Environmental rules restrict the use of traditional antiknocks.
Refiners developed new antiknocks to meet air-quality standards.

senses.definition: Opposed to labor unions or workers’ rights, or harmful to workers’ interests.
examples: Union leaders condemned the bill as deeply antilabor.
Antilabor policies weakened protections for part-time workers.

senses.definition: A person who opposes labor unions or policies that favor workers.
examples: Antilaborists argued that unions limited economic growth.
The party attracted many antilaborists among business owners.

senses.definition: Opposed to the game of lacrosse or to its promotion.
examples: Some parents took an antilacrosse position, preferring other sports.
The antilacrosse campaign complained about rough play and injuries.

senses.definition: Someone who is opposed to lacrosse or dislikes the sport strongly.
examples: The antilacrossers argued that the school should fund other activities.
As an antilacrosser, he refused to watch the matches.
